headline: Nation's Leading Electric Utilities Urge Swift Policy Action to Accelerate Transmission, Lower Costs and Meet Growing Demand
summary: The Grid Acceleration Coalition, representing the nation's leading electric utilities, today filed a complaint with the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) urging immediate action to address outdated federal rules that are delaying critical transmission projects by up to 20 months – driving up costs for customers and threatening the nation's ability to meet surging electricity demand. headline: How Investors May Respond To Ameren (AEE) Earnings Strength And New Bond Issuance For Data Center Growth
summary: In recent weeks, Ameren Corporation reported strong Q4 2025 results and earnings growth for 2025, while also raising US$400 million in senior notes and US$450 million in Ameren Missouri first mortgage bonds at interest rates of 4.80% and 5.55%. This mix of solid earnings, active capital raising, and a continued push into infrastructure and clean energy has sharpened the focus on how Ameren’s balance sheet and growth plans align with its long-term risk profile. We’ll now examine how Ameren’s...
